ideas.question: Is Chainlink (LINK) dead? It's down 80% from ATH and doesn't seem to do anything.
ideas.research: Chainlink is the dominant oracle infrastructure provider. Oracles bridge real-world data to smart contracts. LINK is down ~80% from its ATH of ~$52 in 2021. Current price ~$10-12. Collective Shift does not hold LINK in model portfolios. Key points: 1) Oracle infrastructure is critical - DeFi cannot function without reliable price feeds. 2) Chainlink has ~75% market share in oracle services. 3) CCIP (Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol) is their big bet. 4) The token economics are debated - LINK is used to pay node operators but there's inflation pressure. 5) The team holds a significant portion of supply. 6) Price performance has been poor relative to BTC and ETH. 7) Infrastructure plays often underperform in bull markets but the tech is genuinely important.

slides.text: Is Chainlink dead? It's down 80% from ATH. Here's the data.
LINK hit $52 in 2021. Today it's around $10-12. That's roughly an 80% drawdown. Brutal by any measure.
But here's what most people miss: Chainlink controls ~75% of the oracle market. Every major DeFi protocol uses it. The infrastructure is not dead. The price is.
Oracles bridge real-world data to smart contracts. Without them, DeFi literally cannot function. Chainlink's CCIP is their bet on becoming the cross-chain standard.

Disclosure: Collective Shift does not hold LINK in our model portfolios. Infrastructure plays are genuinely important tech but that does not automatically make them good investments. Token economics matter.
The framework: separate 'good technology' from 'good investment.' They are not always the same thing. Follow for more data-driven breakdowns.
